item.title: Potential first Atlantic tropical cyclone of the year developing in the Gulf
item.description: Forecasters say the potential first tropical cyclone of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season could develop into a fully formed storm on Wednesday and bring life-threatening flash flooding.

item.title: Iran says deal with U.S. requires Israeli forces to leave Lebanon
item.description: Iran's foreign minister says Israeli troops can't remain in Lebanon under the pending deal with the U.S.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Detainees moved out of "Alligator Alcatraz" over hurricane concerns, ICE says
item.description: ICE has moved detainees out of a controversial soft-sided detention center in the Florida Everglades known as "Alligator Alcatraz," a spokesperson said, citing safety concerns around hurricane season.

item.title: Georgia and Alabama GOP runoffs and more primaries to watch today
item.description: Voters are casting ballots in four states and the District of Columbia on Tuesday, including the runoff race in Georgia to take on Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff.

item.title: Trump-backed Barry Moore projected to win runoff in Alabama GOP Senate primary
item.description: Rep. Barry Moore won the Republican Senate runoff in Alabama on Tuesday night, CBS News projects, defeating political newcomer and U.S. Navy Seal Jared Hudson.

item.title: Trump-backed Rep. Mike Collins projected to win Georgia GOP Senate primary runoff
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins will face off against Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff in November as Republicans look to Georgia to deliver a key GOP victory that could determine control of the Senate.

item.title: Latest U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 1, leaves 2 survivors, Pentagon says
item.description: Critics have questioned the overall legality of the boat strikes as well as their effectiveness since the U.S. military began them in September 2025.

item.title: Suspect in custody after deadly shooting at Wilmington Hospital
item.description: A suspect was taken into custody in Philadelphia in connection with the shooting at Wilmington Hospital that left one person dead and another injured, police said.

item.title: Rick Jackson wins Georgia GOP gubernatorial nomination, CBS News projects
item.description: Rick Jackson, the billionaire healthcare executive who grew up in poverty and spent time in Atlanta's public housing projects, has won the Republican runoff for Georgia governor, CBS News projects.

item.title: Questions continue as details of U.S.-Iran peace deal remain unknown
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill are calling on the White House to release the text of the tentative deal reached with Iran to end the war. Both Democrats and Republicans have said they are eager to see the details. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Former FBI agent discuses alleged plot to attack White House UFC event
item.description: Law enforcement sources tell CBS News officials allegedly seized weapons, thousands of rounds of ammunition, and tactical gear while conducting search warrants in connection with the foiled plot to attack the UFC event at the White House over the weekend. CBS News security and law enforcement contributor Lance Leising has more.

item.title: Social media users' views on democracy vary by platform, Gallup study reveals
item.description: A new Gallup study reveals that social media users across platforms differ in how they express their views on democracy. Dr. Ellyn Maese, senior researcher at Gallup and author of the study, joins CBS News to unpack the findings.

item.title: Extended interview: Sen. Raphael Warnock on Iran, Georgia redistricting and faith in America
item.description: In this extended interview, CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett sits down with Democratic Sen. Raphael Warnock of Georgia to discuss the Iran war, voting rights and morality in America.

item.title: Trump slams Israel, praises Iran at G7 summit
item.description: President Trump praised Iranian leaders while slamming his greatest ally in the Middle East, Israel, at the G7 summit on Tuesday. Nancy Cordes has more details.

item.title: Wilmington Hospital shooting suspect at large, police say; at least 1 dead and 1 injured
item.description: Police in Delaware are still searching for a suspect who shot two people at Wilmington Hospital on Tuesday, officials said at a news conference. At least one person is dead and another injured.

item.title: Boston embraces "Scottish invasion" for the World Cup
item.description: Forty thousand noisy tourists who drink your beer and clog your bars is normally not any city's idea of a good time, but Boston is loving it. The Scots have arrived to cheer on their first World Cup appearance of this millennium.

item.title: Prospective homeowners delaying purchases amid high prices, high mortgage rates
item.description: The cost of a typical starter home has never been higher. Cristian Benavides has more in "Affordability in America."

item.title: 17 horses killed in barn fire at Saratoga Casino Hotel Harness Track
item.description: The casino said some 350 horses are usually housed in the area, but the fire was contained before it could spread and only affected the one barn.

item.title: Taxpayer money expected to fund half of Trump's White House ballroom project, Washington Post finds
item.description: A new Washington Post report says President Trump's White House ballroom could cost $600 million, with half the cost borne by taxpayers. Sarah Blaskey, an investigative reporter at The Washington Post who helped break the story, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Starter homes now cost $1 million in hundreds of U.S. cities, Zillow finds
item.description: Online real estate marketplace Zillow found that 242 U.S. cities now have starter homes worth $1 million or more, nearly triple the number of cities reported in 2020. The company defines a starter home as one in the lowest third of home values in a given region. CBS News MoneyWatch reporter Megan Cerullo has more.

item.title: How Georgia's GOP candidates plan on tackling affordability
item.description: Voters in Georgia are casting their ballots in two key Republican runoff races on Tuesday. Trump-backed Lieutenant Gov. Burt Jones and billionaire Rick Jackson are vying for the GOP nomination for governor. The winner will face Democrat and former Atlanta Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms in November. Meanwhile, Trump-backed Rep. Mike Collins is facing off with former college football coach Derek Dooley for the GOP nomination for Senate. The winner of that race will take on Sen. Jon Ossoff in November. CBS News correspondent Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: 6/16: CBS Evening News
item.description: FBI foils alleged murder plot at White House UFC fight; President Trump slams Israel, praises Iran at G7 summit.

item.title: 6/16: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: New details after FBI thwarts alleged terror plot; four states and the District of Columbia hold primaries.

item.title: Yum! Brands sells struggling Pizza Hut in $2.7 billion deal
item.description: The sale will split ownership of the pizza chain between a U.S.-based private equity firm and a Chinese restaurant company.

item.title: SNOO baby bassinets maker issued warning by FDA
item.description: The FDA sent a warning letter to Happiest Baby Inc., alleging the company sold some unauthorized products and cited unsanitary conditions.

item.title: 6/15: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Details still slim on U.S.-Iran deal; California Gov. Gavin Newsom says the Justice Department is investigating him and his wife.

item.title: 6/11: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: U.S. and Iran expected to have 60 days to reach a deal upon letter of intent signing; Thune under pressure from Trump to fire Senate parliamentarian.

item.title: The Uplift: Ellie the elephant
item.description: Meet Big Ellie, the elephant mascot of the WNBA's New York Liberty. Plus more heartwarming news.

item.title: Restaurants turning back the clock to lure customers
item.description: Some restaurants are going retro to bring customers into their dining rooms. CBS News' Bradley Blackburn got a taste of how they're putting nostalgia — on the menu.
